title: Fragrant Dense Vietnamese Beef Cutlet Stew
description: A jammy, dense stew with crispy beef cutlets, simmered in a fragrant broth infused with tomato paste, fish sauce, and aromatics, perfect for a brunch mood.
instructions: Pound beef sirloin into 3cm thick cutlets using a mallet or rolling pin.
In a shallow dish combine flour, salt, and pepper; dredge each cutlet until coated.
Beat egg in a bowl; in another shallow dish place panko breadcrumbs.
Sequentially dip each flour-coated cutlet in the beaten egg, then coat with panko.
Heat olive oil in a kadhai over medium heat; sear cutlets on both sides until golden, about 2 minutes per side.
Remove cutlets and set aside; in the same pan, sauté garlic and shallot until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add diced carrot and stir for another 2 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste, fish sauce, and soy sauce, cooking for 1 minute to bloom the flavors.
Pour in water, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to 160°C and simmer for 45 minutes, covering halfway.
Return the seared cutlets to the stew, cover, and cook for an additional 5 minutes to reheat, then serve hot.
